items.title: The War Against Imagination
items.summary: The SAIC cracks down on a professor’s thought experiment and the Obama Presidential Center embodies a nostalgic idea of public life.

items.title: Sarah Schulman’s Four Decades of Lesbian Fiction
items.summary: “Nothing stops me except the publishing industry,” quipped the novelist and AIDS historian, who cut her teeth as an East Village journalist writing for queer and feminist papers.

items.title: Art Movements: Sam Gilliam Foundation Names Its First Director
items.summary: Dr. Steven Nelson to helm the nonprofit, Aperture HQ's fall opening date, and, uh, the New Museum partners with Penske Media?

items.title: Required Reading
items.summary: This week: Jean Shin’s memorial to the trees of Greenwood Cemetery, the 250th anniversary nobody wants, Pride bar-hopping, and more.
